description:
Goal: prove recursive convergence and root retraction as ordinary fact behavior.

Change:
- Extend activation identity from exact first-pass inputs to finite canonical keys with widening/join policy for recursive SCCs.
- Track root-scoped semantic ownership so a changed lowered body can remove old callsite/callee/activation/executable contributions when they are no longer derived.
- Make `CheckSemanticClosure` account for changed/retracted semantic facts and publish the next closed root revision only after the root-scoped semantic frontier is stable.
- Preserve root-independent cached definitions and lowered bodies while excluding unreachable semantic islands from the new `SemanticClosed` set.

Why:
- Quicksort and reducer loops must converge without hiding a second type-inference loop behind a job.
- Redefining `main/0` from qsort to `foo/0` should retract qsort/partition/append from the root by contribution replacement, not by recompiling the whole program.

Checks:
- Quicksort `qsort/1` and `partition/4` settle with finite activation keys and stable return facts.
- Redefining uncalled `foo/0` does not reopen the quicksort root.
- Redefining `main/0` to call `foo/0` instead of qsort removes qsort/partition/append activation/executable facts from the next semantic closure and adds `foo/0`.
- Tests assert retractions and unchanged blast radius through telemetry/job effects.
title: Compiler2 semantic: support recursive convergence and root retractions
